image_prompt: SVG: two-panel water-clock graphs. Graph 1: scatter+inverse curve of Time/s (y 0-35, ticks every 5) vs Area/cm² (x 0-2.5, ticks every 0.5), t = 15/area decreasing from 30 s at 0.5 cm² to ~7.5 s at 2.0 cm². Graph 2 (titled 'Variation of fill time with the inverse of different areas of hole'): straight line through origin, Time/s (y 0-35) vs Inverse of area/cm⁻² (x 0-2.5), gradient 15 s·cm², points (0.5,7.5) to (2.0,30).

SVG: four multiple-choice graph-shape sketches (no numeric axes), labelled A-D. A: straight line through origin (directly proportional). B: upward-curving line, increasing gradient (concave up). C: decreasing decay curve toward zero. D: straight line with positive y-intercept and negative gradient.

SVG titled 'Variation of time to empty the water tube with initial height of water in the tube': scatter + best-fit straight line, Time/s (y 0-16, ticks every 2) vs Initial height of water/m (x 0-0.6, ticks every 0.1). Line has positive y-intercept ~5.0 s (NOT through origin); points (0.1,6.4),(0.2,9.0),(0.3,11.1),(0.4,13.0),(0.5,14.2).

SVG: four student-method graphs A-D, each Time/s (y 0-35) vs Initial height of water/m (x 0-0.6). Method A: steep straight line over narrow low-height range (0.05-0.11 m). Method B: straight line, y-intercept ~11, points clustered 0.18-0.28 m. Method C: straight line, y-intercept ~15, points clustered 0.40-0.52 m. Method D (most valid): full-range square-root curve t≈40√h covering 0.02-0.58 m, showing the true non-linear relationship.

SVG titled 'Electrical energy consumption between 1975 and 2005 – China compared to the world average': two rising line series, Electrical energy consumption / kWh per person per year (y 0-3500, ticks every 500) vs Year (x 1970-2010, ticks every 5). World average rises ~1350 (1975) to ~2600 (2005). China rises ~700 (1975) to ~3050 (2005), crossing above the world average around 2000. Key distinguishes China and World average.

SVG titled 'Population of China between 1975 and 2005': single rising line, Population / billions (y 0.80-1.30, ticks every 0.05) vs Year (x 1975-2005, ticks every 5). Population rises from ~0.83 billion (1975) to ~1.26 billion (2005).

SVG: four multiple-choice bar-magnet field-line diagrams A-D, each magnet red N (top) / dark S (bottom). A: radial straight arrows pointing outward (incorrect). B: closed field loops leaving the N pole and entering the S pole, arrows pointing away from N (correct). C: same loops but arrows reversed, pointing into N (incorrect). D: nested concentric closed ovals around the whole magnet (incorrect).

SVG: transformer circuit on a toroidal iron core. Primary coil P (left, 10 turns, copper windings) connected to a Power supply 110 V battery (+/-). Secondary coil S (right, 20 turns) connected to an ammeter (A circle). Core labelled P and S.
SVG data table: columns Primary coil / Secondary coil; rows Voltage (24 V / 12 V) and Current (2.50 A / 4.75 A). Shaded teal header row, clean ruled grid.

SVG: two side-by-side illustrative panels. Left: outdoor real snow slope with pine trees on the ridge and a skier descending. Right: indoor floodlit ski slope (dark ceiling, overhead lamps, ski-lift pylon, distant skiers, foreground pine).
SVG: ski-slope ramp diagram with vertical height marked 50 m and slope length marked 205 m (red dimension arrows), plus a skier silhouette holding skis labelled 'Mass of skier 52 kg'.
SVG: two ski-slope ramps side by side, labelled 'A: Real snow slope' and 'B: Indoor ski slope', each a snow-covered ramp with a start hut at the top.

render_data.title: Water-clock simulation — time to empty each tube
render_data.variable: Initial height of water in the tube
render_data.metric: Time to empty the tube / s
render_data.task: Press Water release to start the tubes draining; read the stopwatch for the 8 cm tube. Answer: 12.8 s.
render_data.trend: Time to empty increases with initial height, but not in proportion (a square-root-like relationship).

image_prompt: SVG: water-clock simulation panel. Tabs 'Water simulation' / 'Final time 8 cm'. 'Water release' Start button. Row of five small analog stopwatches (all reading zero). Five water tubes with taps, water heights labelled 6 cm, 10 cm, 2 cm, 8 cm, 4 cm. Brace connecting to five answer boxes reading 0.0 s, 0.0 s, 0.0 s, ? (highlighted), 0.0 s.
SVG: large analog stopwatch, 60-second dial (labels 60/5/10/15/.../55), red hand reading 12.8 s (between 10 and 15), title 'Final time 8 cm'.

render_data.title: Energy transformations in a hydroelectric power plant
render_data.prompt: Drag the forms of energy into the correct order of transformation that takes place in a hydroelectric power plant.

image_prompt: SVG: cross-section of a hydroelectric facility. Right: Reservoir behind a Dam. Penstock pipe carries water (flow arrow) down to a Turbine, connected by a shaft to a Generator, then a Transformer, then Power output to the left. Tailrace river outflow at bottom-left with flow arrow.
